questions.question_text: What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.student_answer: The main idea is that the boy is excited about his new school.

questions.question_text: What does the boy do on his first day of school?
questions.student_answer: He meets his teacher and classmates.

questions.question_text: Why is the boy excited?
questions.student_answer: He is excited because he gets to see his new school and make new friends.

questions.question_text: What does the boy do after school?
questions.student_answer: He goes home and tells his parents about his day.

questions.question_text: What is the boy's favorite part of the day?
questions.student_answer: His favorite part is playing with his new friends during recess.

questions.question_text: What is the opposite of 'tall'?
What is the opposite of 'happy'?
What is the opposite of 'hot'?
What is the opposite of 'fast'?
What is the opposite of 'big'?
Write a sentence using the word 'beautiful'.
questions.student_answer: The garden is beautiful with colorful flowers.

questions.question_text: Write a sentence using the word 'quick'.
questions.student_answer: The rabbit ran quick across the field.

questions.question_text: Write a sentence using the word 'loud'.
questions.student_answer: The music was loud and everyone danced.

questions.question_text: Write a sentence using the word 'clean'.
questions.student_answer: The classroom is clean and tidy.

questions.question_text: Write a sentence using the word 'kind'.
questions.student_answer: My teacher is kind and always helps me.

questions.question_text: Write a short story about a day at the park.
questions.student_answer: It was a sunny day and I went to the park with my family. We played on the swings and slid down the slide. I fed the ducks in the pond and they quacked loudly. We had a picnic and ate sandwiches and fruit. I felt very happy and tired when we went home. I can't wait to go back to the park again.

questions.question_text: Name the four major food groups.
What are carbohydrates?
What are proteins?
What are fats?
What do we need food for?
How many major groups of food that are mentioned in the passage?
Which of the following is NOT one of the food groups mentioned in the passage?
What is the primary function of carbohydrates in our bodies?
Which of these foods is an example of carbohydrate?
Proteins are mainly responsible for:
Which of the following foods is rich in protein?
Fats help our bodies by:
Vitamins assist in:
Fruits and vegetables are primary sources of:
Which of the following is NOT a role of water in our bodies?
Which of the following is a way to take water into our bodies?
A balanced diet means:
What can happen to you if you eat food from only one group?
questions.student_answer: B) You may not grow the way you are supposed to.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a function of vitamins?
Cassava is an example of:
What are the two functions of vitamins?
questions.student_answer: To boost immunity and strengthen bones. To heal wounds and improve eyesight.

questions.question_text: How can we get water into our bodies?
questions.student_answer: To drink water. To eat fruits.

questions.question_text: Complete the story below by picking the correct words from the box to fill the blank spaces.
My father is a ... (farmer)
Our school has many... (teacher) who are very kind.
I shall be a wonderful... (read) when I practice reading more.
My mother likes cooking. She is a nice... (cook).
Cyamamare owns a large business. He is a... (trade) in town.
What does 'weather' mean?
What does 'temperature' tell us?
It seems to be... (your, yours).
Is ... at the gate? (she, her)
These are ... (our, ours) knives and these are ... (your, yours).
Do ... (they, them) want to come with us?

Complete the following paragraph by using the verbs in brackets in the correct forms. Yesterday, Anna (wake).....up early. She (eat).....breakfast and (go).....to school. After school, she (play).....with her friends and (do).....her homework. In the evening, she (watch).....TV and (call).....her grandma. Tomorrow Anna will do three things. She (help).....her mum cook dinner. She (clean).....her room and she (study).....for her test.
questions.student_answer: woke, ate, went, played, did, watched, called, will help, will clean, will study

questions.question_text: New York is ... than Washington. (Write this adjective correctly and complete the sentence: good)
He dressed smartly. He wanted to attend the party. (Join these two sentences with ...in order to...)
Tom is very intelligent. He cannot fail this test. (Join the two sentences using: ...too....to...)
questions.student_answer: Tom is too intelligent to fail the test

questions.question_text: I like watching movies than reading stories. (Use: prefer...to)
questions.student_answer: I prefer watching movies to reading stories

questions.question_text: Kigali is a nice city. (Add a question tag)
questions.student_answer: Kigali is a nice city, isn't it?

You... (must, mustn't) play with fire.
You... (must, mustn't) be at home on time.
You... (must, mustn't) cheat in a test.

In not more than 150 words, discuss the characteristics of good friends.
questions.student_answer: A good friend is a person with whom you share everything. He does not betray you when you share secrets. The good friend is always there to assist when in need. He thinks about you when there is an opportunity of promotion or a chance for development. A good friend will always be by your side in difficulties and in good times. He suffers with you and rejoices with you.
